cards.front: What is the Nearest Centroid Classifier?
cards.back: A machine learning algorithm that classifies data points into classes based on their similarity to the centroid of each class.

cards.front: How does the Nearest Centroid Classifier work?
cards.back: It calculates the centroid of each class by averaging the features of all data points in that class, and then classifies new data points as the class with the closest centroid.

cards.front: What is the centroid calculated from?
cards.back: The centroid is calculated from the mean of all feature values for each class.

cards.front: What is the advantage of using the Nearest Centroid Classifier?
cards.back: It is simple to implement and can be effective for linearly separable data.

cards.front: What is the disadvantage of the Nearest Centroid Classifier?
cards.back: It can be inaccurate for non-linearly separable data and may be sensitive to outliers.
